title: A 'valid' email can score low — the 0-100 score penalizes catch-all / disposable / role
summary: SpiderVerify returns each email with a status (valid/risky/invalid/unknown) AND a 0-100 score. The score starts at 50 and penalizes catch-all (-15), disposable (-30), and role accounts (-10), so a syntactically valid, MX-present, deliverable address on a catch-all domain lands in the 60s and a valid disposable can score ~30. status:valid + a mid score is not a contradiction — explain it from the quality flags. Verification is also deliberately slow (rate-limited per email to protect deliverability), so a company with many emails adds real time; cap max_emails when you only need a few.
references: docs/services/spiderverifyer/LEARNINGS.md:164-169 — 3-second delay between verifications is critical (slow by design)
